titleStmt.title: Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 621

p.seg: Alexander, by the grace of God king of the Scots, to all worthy men of the whole of his kingdom, greeting.

Let present and future know us to have granted and made firm by this our charter the donation which William, son of Robert of Scremerston, with the consent of William his heir, made to God, Saint Cuthbert, adn Richard the Prior and the Convent of Coldingham and their successors, in respect of his whole land of Prenderguest, with two tofts and crofts, and with all its pertinents, without any withholding. For the same prior and convent of Coldingham to hold and to have for ever of the said William and his heirs, with all its pertinents and easements, as freely, quitely, fully, and honourably as the charter of the said William drawn up on the matter for the same prior and convent justly bears witness; saving our service.
As witnesses: William, earl of Mar; Bernard of Ripley; William of Mordington; William Gifford; Geoffrey of Farsley.
At Berwick on the sixth day of October in the thirty-first year of the lord king's reign [1245]
